message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  As we calculate analytic to link the coefficient of third-order polynomial equations from raw data of an Asean to the SEIR model. The Reproductive index depending on the average incubation period and the average infection period and the coefficient polynomial equations fitted from raw are derived. We also consider the difference of the average incubation period as 5 days and 3 days with the average infection period as 10 day of an Asean. We find that the value of
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  are Indonesia (7.97), Singapore (6.22), Malaysia (3.86), Thailand (2.48), respectively. And we also find that Singapore has 2 values of
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  as 1.54 (16 Feb to 37 March) and 6.22 (31 March-4 April).The peak of infection rate are not found for Singapore and Indonesia at the time of consideration. The model of external stimulus is added into raw data of Singapore and Indonesia to find the maximum rate of infection. We find that Singapore need more magnitude of external stimulus than Indonesia. And the external stimulus for 14 days can stimulate to occur the peak of infected daily case of both country.
                </jats:p>
title: The reproductive index from SEIR model of Covid-19 epidemic in Asean

reference.unstructured: Okhuese V.A. (2020). Mathematical predictions for Covid-19 as a global pandemic. https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.03.19.20038794doi
Inaba, H. (2017). Age-Structured Populatin Dynamics in Demography and Epidemiology; Springer: Berlin/Heidelberg, Germany.

reference.article-title: Incubation period and other epidemiological characteristics of 2019 novel coronavirus infections with right truncation: A statistical analysis of publicly available case data

reference.article-title: Reproduction numbers and sub- threshold endemic equilibria for compartmental models of disease transmission
An active pulse transmission line simulating nerve axon
